para: The GlobalSat DG-388 is a USB mass-storage GPS data logger built around the Airoha AG3335M multi-GNSS chipset. It records track logs to its internal flash memory as .gpl files containing fixed 28-byte binary records with no file header.

Each record stores date, time (UTC), latitude, longitude, altitude, speed, heading, and a record-type flag. Coordinates are stored as signed 32-bit integers in units of 1e-7 degrees (the same convention used by Garmin FIT files). Altitude is in decimeters, speed in hundredths of km/h, and heading in hundredths of degrees.

The record-type flag distinguishes automatic track log entries (flag=2) from manual waypoints recorded by pressing the device's button (flag=3). Manual waypoints have no name field; GPSBabel assigns sequential names (WPT-001, WPT-002, etc.).

This format is read-only. The DG-388 presents its flash storage as a standard USB mass-storage device, so .gpl files can be copied directly from the device. The device can also output live NMEA sentences at 4800 baud via its USB-serial interface, which can be read using the NMEA format with a serial connection.
This format has no configurable options.
